title: epithymum arvense
intro: Epithymum arvense, also known as Epithymum densiflorum, is a type of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is commonly known as creeping thyme, although it is not to be confused with the more commonly known culinary herb thyme. Epithymum arvense has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and promoting relaxation.
